articles.headline: Amazon-backed X-energy Files to Raise up to $800M in IPO
articles.summary: Nuclear startup X-energy, backed by Amazon, launches IPO to fund advanced reactor tech addressing data center energy needs for AI growth. This signals rising infrastructure investments to mitigate power shortages, potentially stabilizing cloud and AI compute costs long-term.

articles.headline: AI Energy Demands Strain Computing Resources, Leading to Rationing
articles.summary: AI companies face acute energy shortages forcing rationing of computing power, slowing deployment timelines and pressuring Big Tech to invest in energy-efficient infrastructure. This exposes bottlenecks in data centers that threaten AI scalability and raise compute costs, widening the competitive moat for incumbents over startups.

articles.headline: Nvidia-backed SiFive Hits $3.65 Billion Valuation for Open AI Chips
articles.summary: SiFive's RISC-V based chips for AI applications reach $3.65B valuation, offering an open-source alternative amid surging demand for specialized hardware. This could reduce dependency on proprietary architectures, impacting chip supply chains and hardware costs for AI infrastructure.
